text.title: When ui-ux-design.md is required vs skipped
text.summary: The artefact is conditional on what the feature changes. The conditions are intentionally tight — over-application produces audit theatre.

list.item: Web UI — page, component, form, button, modal, navigation.
Mobile UI — screen, sheet, gesture handler.
CLI TUI — interactive prompt, picker, dashboard view.

rule.statement: If the feature renders or modifies any visible element on the listed surfaces, ui-ux-design.md MUST exist.
rule.rationale: Heuristic gaps are invisible in code review; the artefact is the only forcing function.

list.item: API-only — REST / GraphQL contracts with no client change.
Data migrations — pure DB schema work.
Internal refactor — input and output unchanged.
Build-system / CI / infra — no rendered surface.

rule.statement: When skipped, readiness.md item 7 MUST be marked "no UI impact" with a one-line reason.
rule.rationale: Silent skip turns the gate into a rubber stamp. Explicit skip leaves an audit trail.

antipattern.description: Filing ui-ux-design.md for a backend feature "just in case".
antipattern.reason: Pads the spec with empty audit rows; trains reviewers to skim over the artefact when it does matter.
